description: Figure 1 presents a schematic illustration of the selective laser melting (SLM) setup used to fabricate Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel from spherical Fe30Mn9AlC3Ni0.6Cr powder. The main panel depicts the powder-bed configuration with a laser beam scanning over the build platform, a powder feed container, and an overflow container, indicating the moving direction of the feed mechanism. The lower-left inset details the scanning strategy, showing a 67° rotation between adjacent layers (Layer n and Layer n+1) to minimize laser vector coincidence and crystallographic texture. The lower-right inset provides a cross-sectional view of the melt pool, illustrating the interaction between the laser beam and the powder bed atop the fabricated part and substrate. The figure establishes the experimental framework for samples printed at laser powers of 70–150 W, corresponding to energy densities of 58.33–125.00 J/mm³, with a scanning speed of 750 mm/s, layer thickness of 0.02 mm, and hatch spacing of 0.08 mm.
